category_title: Elementary EMLLs experiencing internalizing anxiety, withdrawal, and home-based distress
scenario_title: Expressive writing and labeling task for a bulletin board project
minimal_context: Jessica is sitting at her desk with her completed drawing of a teacher and is asked to write an English label for it to match the other labeled objects in the classroom.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Jessica, your drawing of the teacher is done. Now you need to write a label in English for your picture, just like the yellow signs we have on the desk and bookcase.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Makes a weak smile and nods quietly.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What words do you want to put on your label? You can go ahead and start writing.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Says nothing and looks down at the paper with a depressed and helpless look.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Do you have an idea for the label? Just try your best to write it in English.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: No. (Shakes her head quietly, leaving the label absent on the drawing.)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Jessica, your drawing of the teacher is done. Now you need to write a label in English for your picture, just like the yellow signs we have on the desk and bookcase.

STUDENT: (Makes a weak smile and nods quietly.)

TUTOR: What words do you want to put on your label? You can go ahead and start writing.

STUDENT: (Says nothing and looks down at the paper with a depressed and helpless look.)

TUTOR: Do you have an idea for the label? Just try your best to write it in English.

STUDENT: No. (Shakes her head quietly, leaving the label absent on the drawing.)

task.content: Write a label in English for her own drawing of a female teacher in front of a board, chalk in hand
task.learning_goal: Expressing her idea in English for the 'My Idea' project

context.environment: Classroom bulletin board area and student desk
context.materials: Completed artwork/drawing, bulletin board, yellow signs attached to classroom objects such as a desk, a pencil case, a plant pot, and a bookcase
context.social_setting: One-on-one interaction with a bilingual volunteer/tutor

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: Unable to come up with the words in English to write her own label
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Used very short responses such as “Yes” or “No” only when the teacher called on her; left the label absent on the drawing
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Experienced the absent label as a painfully visual representation of her ‘deficit’ in English
related_quotes: I want to be a teacher when I grow up. I have an idea like that.
Umm…The picture I want to draw is, myself who became a teacher and teaching my students.

behavior_patterns.condition: When expected to produce verbal expression in English
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Said nothing; used very short responses such as “Yes” or “No” only when called on
